methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
ANYbotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 9 private robotics firms at the same stage plus 1 public comparable, providing triangulation across two methods.

The Stage-Progression method, which applies a typical 1.9x multiplier to the $50M capital raised as a valuation proxy, yields $95M. The Peer-Median approach examines six same-sector, same-stage companies—1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M)—producing a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneous investor appetite and technology differentiation within frontier robotics.

Triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $81M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $585M. The substantial gap between methods reflects structural uncertainty: Stage-Progression is capital-efficiency driven and conservative, while Peer-Median is market-driven but sensitive to outlier valuations such as 1X Technologies. Key uncertainties include ANYbotics' commercial traction relative to better-funded peers, technology defensibility in a crowded Series B cohort, and sustainability of elevated valuations in the robotics sector absent near-term path to profitability.
